race.comment: Onto the Grand Annual Steeplechase with 5500m of stamina over the big fences. DUKE OF BEDFORD (2) won this race in dominant fashion last year, leading throughout to score by 12 lengths having also won the Brierly on the Tuesday. Andrew Bobbin elected not to run Tuesday this year and focus on the Grand Annual - we'll back him in there and if his latest steeple trial is anything to go by, he's ready to rumble. HIT THE ROAD JACK (6) was the best of those backing up from Tuesday's Brierly, given a good betting push behind the champ Stern Idol and battling on well for second. He's unproven at the longer trips but you wouldn't want him in any other camp to do so. THE MIGHTY SPAR (3) was fourth on Tuesday having chased home Stern Idol over 3850m prior as well. He's a chance. RAISE THE NOTE (10) is yet to win a jumps race but 2nd to INSTIGATOR last time ties a lot of these in. LEADERBOARD (1) has won dominantly out to 4500m in the Grand National and should beat a few home on stamina.

Live1.SchedulerNote: *P1 [FEATURED Race] Warrnambool May Racing Carnival - Prize Money: $350,000
